LAW AND MOTION 302, MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSE TO REQUEST, DEMAND INSPECTION, PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS IS GRANTED (INCLUSIVE OF ALL INSPECTION DEMANDS CITED IN PLAINTIFF'S MOTION). AS TO THE INSPECTION DEMANDS, DEFENDANTS OR THEIR COUNSEL SHOULD IMMEDIATELY PROVIDE NOTICE TO MESSERS. SCHANTZ AND DAVIS OF THE INSPECTION DEMANDS, RPD'S 21-24 (IF THEY HAVE NOT ALREADY DONE SO). ADDITIONALLY, SUBSTANTIVE, WRITTEN RESPONSES IN CONFORMANCE WITH CCP MUST BE SUBMITTED BY DEFENDANTS AS TO THE EXISTENCE OF ANY DOCUMENTS CONSTITUTING OR REFERRING TO ANY SUCH PERFORMANCE REVIEWS OR DISCIPLINARY ACTIONS TAKEN. AS TO THE PRIVILEGE LOG, THE CLAIM OF PRIVILEGE AS TO THE 4/11/12 DOCUMENT IS OVERRULED. SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED IN FAVOR OF PLAINTIFF AND AGAINST DEFENSE COUNSEL IN THE REDUCED AMOUNT OF $1,400.00. PARTIES ARE TO MEET AND CONFER RE: THE PREPARATION AND SUBMISSION OF A PROPOSED ORDER. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: JAIME HOPP, CSR #13751 (TEL NO. 925-759-0770); DEPT. 302. (302/EHG)

DISCOVERY 302, THE PARTIES DID NOT STIPULATE TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM. PLAINTIFF OSMAN KARAKUS' MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER RE: DEFENDANT MG-252 CAL, LLC AND THE MINA GROUP, LLC'S NOTICE OF FURTHER DEPOSITION OF PLAINTIFF IS CONTINUED TO FEBRUARY 4, 2015 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302. THE JUDGE PRO TEM SHALL PREPARE A REPORT AND RECOMMENDATION. JUDGE PRO TEM: DAVID HELBRAUN; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF OSMAN KARAKUS' MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER RE: DEFENDANT MG-252 CAL, LLC AND THE MINA GROUP, LLC'S NOTICE OF FURTHER DEPOSITION OF PLAINTIFF IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. THE COURT WILL ALLOW DEFENDANTS TO DEPOSE PLAINTIFF FOR TWO HOURS, LIMITED TO THE ISSUE OF DAMAGES AND WITH NO DUPLICATION. THE COURT FINDS AN AWARD OF SANCTIONS TO BE JUSTIFIED. SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED IN FAVOR OF PLAINTIFF AND AGAINST DEFENSE COUNSEL IN THE AMOUNT OF $2,000.00 PURSUANT TO CCP 2025.420(H). PARTIES ARE TO MEET AND CONFER RE: THE PREPARATION AND SUBMISSION OF A PROPOSED ORDER. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: JAIME HOPP, CSR #13751 (TEL NO. 925-759-0770); DEPT. 302. (302/EHG)

MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSE TO REQUEST, DEMAND INSPECTION, PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS
Off Calendar Feb-04-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. ALYCE FOSHEE of Ari Law, PC, Tel No. (415) 357-3600 appearing for Plaintiff Osman Karakus;
Atty. MICHAEL D. MCCONATHY of Lynch, Gilardi & Grummer, APC, Tel No. (415) 397-2800 appearing for Defendants MG-252 Cal, LLC & The Mina Group, LLC.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court modified its tentative ruling as to the issue of sanctions, and ruled as follows: PLAINTIFF OSMAN KARAKUS' MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSE TO REQUEST, DEMAND INSPECTION, PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS is granted (inclusive of all inspection demands cited in Plaintiff's motion). As to the inspection demands, Defendants or their counsel should immediately provide notice to Messers. Schantz and Davis of the inspection demands, RPD's 21-24 (if they have not already done so). Additionally, substantive, written responses in conformance with CCP must be submitted by Defendants as to the existence of any documents constituting or referring to any such performance reviews or disciplinary actions taken. The existence of such records is discoverable and the need of discovery by plaintiff to seek admissible evidence to prove his case on the merits is a compelling interest. As to the privilege log, the claim of privilege as to the 4/11/12 document is overruled. Sanctions are awarded in favor of plaintiff and against defense counsel in the reduced amount of $1,400.00. Parties are to meet and confer re: the preparation and submission of a proposed order. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Jaime Hopp, CSR #13751 (Tel No. 925-759-0770) (302/EHG)

Plaintiff Osman Karakus' Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Re: Defendant Mg-252 Cal, Llc And The Mina Group, Llc'S Notice Of Further Deposition Of Plaintiff
Off Calendar Feb-04-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. ALYCE FOSHEE of Ari Law, PC, Tel No. (415) 357-3600 appearing for Plaintiff Osman Karakus;
Atty. MICHAEL D. MCCONATHY of Lynch, Gilardi & Grummer, APC, Tel No. (415) 397-2800 appearing for Defendants MG-252 CAL, LLC & THE MINA GROUP, LLC

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court modified its tentative ruling, and ruled as follows: Plaintiff Osman Karakus' Motion For Protective Order Re: Defendant MG-252 Cal, LLC And The Mina Group, LLC'S Notice Of Further Deposition Of Plaintiff's is granted in part and denied in part. The Court will allow Defendants to depose Plaintiff for two hours, limited to the issue of damages and with no duplication. The Court finds an award of sanctions to be justified. Sanctions are awarded in favor of plaintiff and against defense counsel in the amount of $2,000.00 pursuant to CCP 2025.420(h). Parties are to meet and confer re: the preparation and submission of a proposed order. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Jaime Hopp, CSR #13751 (Tel No. 925-759-0770) (302/EHG)
